# Documentation Contribution Guide

## Purpose

This guide explains how GitHub contributors should propose, review, and publish AIFlow OS Engineering Handbook changes.

## Contribution Flow

1. Read `docs/MASTER_CONTEXT.md`, `docs/ROADMAP.md`, `docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`, `docs/CONSTITUTION.md`, and `docs/STANDARDS.md`.
2. Open an issue for substantial new content, terminology changes, ADR proposals, roadmap changes, diagrams, or review requests.
3. Reference affected files and expected outcomes.
4. Draft the pull request using the pull request template.
5. Update `CHANGELOG.md`, `docs/MASTER_CONTEXT.md`, and `docs/ROADMAP.md` when required.
6. Respond to review comments with specific edits or documented rationale.

## Review Expectations

Use `docs/REVIEW_PROCESS.md` for architecture, technical, editorial, consistency, quality, final approval, and publishing approval.

## Quality Bar

Documentation should be complete enough for a future engineer to use. Avoid vague assertions, generic lists, unsupported comparisons, and placeholder sections.

## GitHub Labels

The repository uses labels to classify documentation, architecture, ADR, glossary, roadmap, security, quality, and review work. Label definitions are stored in `.github/labels.yml`.
